reason: The statement applies because the text describes how Josiah, the king of Judah, found the book of the law in the house of the LORD and upon hearing its words realized the wrath of the LORD was kindled against them for forsaking Him and worshiping other gods. Josiah takes actions to remove idolatry and follow the commandments of the LORD, showing repentance for the sins of his fathers. Despite Josiah's efforts, the text indicates that the LORD's great wrath was still kindled against Judah due to past provocations.
